input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi scheme tuned for a young family: pared-back Scandinavian lines meet Japanese restraint. Emphasize low-profile furniture, natural woods, woven textures and layered neutrals. Keep negative space and simple focal points (rattan pendant over dining, a low coffee table in the living bay) while integrating a compact work corner and a safe, minimal crib in the bedroom.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals (#EDE7DF, #CBB698, #A88461) form the base to create a soothing backdrop; mid tones (#6D6A5F) for furniture and anchoring elements; deep accent (#2B2724) used sparingly for metal details and contrast. This palette supports soft natural light and layered textures without feeling monochrome.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft ambient daylight with warm, dimmable accents in the evening. Prioritize layered lighting: diffuse ceiling/pendant for general mood, warm directional task light at the desk and bedside, and low-level floor/lantern lighting for the tea-nook/relax areas.
furniture_language: visual horizontality (long bench, low coffee table)
subtle rounded edges for family-friendly safety
styling_rules: Keep surfaces mostly clear — one sculptural object or a single ceramic vase per major surface.
Layer 2 rugs in living/tea-nook zones (natural-fiber base + soft wool top).
Use plants sparingly to add life and scale: single taller plant + 1–2 small pots.
Balance open negative space with a single warm focal pendant in the dining bay.
Integrate the work corner visually by using matching wood tones and a linen pinboard.
avoid: bright saturated colors or bold patterns
excessive visual clutter or too many small decorative items
large, high-back sectionals that block sightlines

room_guidance.headline: Calm family bedroom with safe minimal crib and soft textures
room_guidance.guidance: Place a simple birch-slat crib along the exterior wall under the window (leave 18–24 in clearance). Main bed should be low-profile on a wooden base oriented to preserve the window sightline. Include a compact wardrobe/dresser and a small, soft-glow bedside lamp. Introduce a washable low-pile rug to create a cozy play/diaper area. Keep furniture edges rounded for child safety.
must_include: crib (birch or light oak slats, minimal)
must_avoid: heavy dark furniture that dominates the room

room_guidance.headline: Low, layered Japandi living with integrated standing desk work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Orient low sofa toward the glass door to Relax; place a low wooden coffee table and one floor cushion/tea-nook element near the dining bay to carry the theme across. Tuck a compact standing desk (light oak, narrow) along an interior wall or near the dining threshold to create a cozy work corner — use a linen pinboard and a warm task lamp. Add one taller plant near the sliding door and a tactile wool rug to ground the seating.
must_include: low coffee table (round or rectangular, oak)
compact standing desk (narrow, light oak) with task lamp
one potted plant near sliding door
must_avoid: bulky entertainment units that block light paths

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining bay with rattan pendant focal point
room_guidance.guidance: Use a round oak dining table sized for 4 and light linen-upholstered chairs. Hang a woven rattan pendant centered above the table at comfortable height (approx 30 in from table). Keep table setting minimal: one stoneware vase or bowl, neutral placemats. Leverage bay windows as a daylight focal, keep window treatments light (sheer linen).
must_avoid: large buffet or tall cabinets blocking bay light

room_guidance.headline: Minimal, functional kitchen that reads warm and uncluttered
room_guidance.guidance: Keep cabinetry in warm neutral tones, hide appliances where possible. Open shelving for a few ceramics and wooden cutting boards preserves the aesthetic. If counter space is limited, stage a single wooden chopping board and a small potted herb. Ensure visual connection to dining bay for light flow.
must_include: wooden chopping board and 1–2 ceramic pieces

room_guidance.headline: Spa-like small bathroom: warm surfaces and simple fittings
room_guidance.guidance: Use clay-toned tiles or plaster walls, a slim wooden vanity and a round mirror. Keep toiletries minimal and stage a wooden bath tray and a folded linen towel. If tub exists, add a small ceramic soap dish and a narrow high window should remain unobstructed.
must_avoid: brightly colored towels or bath mats

room_guidance.headline: Compact laundry with tidy, utility-first styling
room_guidance.guidance: Staged with stacked washer/dryer (if possible) or compact units, a slim folding counter and woven laundry basket. Keep door closed in shots showing low light to avoid cluttered interiors.
must_avoid: random detergents or bottles on counters

room_guidance.headline: Small outdoor patio for quiet mornings — weatherproof natural materials
room_guidance.guidance: Use a low wooden bench or rattan seating with a weatherproof cushion, a small side table and potted bamboo or grasses. Stage sliding door slightly open to blur indoor-outdoor boundary. Keep decor minimal and breathable for family use.

room_guidance.headline: Decluttered circulation with subtle storage cues
room_guidance.guidance: Show narrow console or shelf with single tray for keys and a woven shoe basket. Keep walls sparse with one small round mirror or wood peg rail.

room_guidance.headline: Organized, minimal closet staging
room_guidance.guidance: Use neutral hanging textiles, folded linen on shelves and a small storage box. Keep door slightly ajar for depth but avoid overfilling.

room_guidance.headline: Entry storage — simple and functional
room_guidance.guidance: A small bench with shoe storage below and a basket for bags. Keep styling minimal for quick access staging.

room_guidance.headline: Outdoor storage — tidy and weatherproof
room_guidance.guidance: Store outdoor cushions and small garden tools in a woven trunk or lidded box. Keep objects tidy, not staged as piled.

warnings: Only measured dimensions supplied for Dream, Nest and Dine. Confirm precise furniture clearances if exact staging is required.
Crib placement must follow local safety codes — leave appropriate clearance from windows and blinds.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Warm, calm Japandi — breathable, low, and family-friendly
global_style_notes: Prioritize horizontal lines and low furniture to reinforce Japandi calm.
Use a restrained decorative palette and one strong natural-material focal (rattan pendant or wooden bench).
Integrate the standing desk visually — matching wood tone and minimal accessories to prevent it feeling like an afterthought.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: no bright saturated colors, no heavy ornate/traditional furniture, no industrial chrome fixtures, no cluttered surfaces, no large high-back sectionals, avoid visible laundry piles and toys scattered across main sightlines

evals.message: Strong match to the specification: birch/light-oak crib positioned under the exterior window, low wooden bed with linen bedding, low washable-style rug, warm bedside lamp, and a standing desk are all present. Natural daylight plus warm lamp is realistic; scale and architecture are consistent and marketable. Minor nit: a wheeled office chair is slightly more utilitarian than pure Japandi but does not compromise the listing quality. Approved for further image-to-video generation.
